user.title: Set up IDM Navigator Heat Pump
user.description:
[Need Help? Read the Documentation]({wiki_url})

Enter the connection details of your IDM heat pump.

**Required for full operation:** On the IDM Navigator/controller, enable *Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP → On (Ein)*. Usually use port **502** and slave ID **1**. If the menu is missing or locked, your heating installer or iDM service must enable it.

**Optional local web access:** Is a *Local network code* configured on the Navigator? Set it on the controller display under *Settings → General settings → Network settings → Local network code*. Cloud/app passwords and two-factor authentication codes are not used here; an empty value or `0` disables local web access.

data.name: Name
data.web_host: Original heat pump IP for web access

data_description.name: Display name for the device in Home Assistant, e.g. "IDM Heat Pump".
data_description.host: IP address or hostname used for Modbus TCP. If you use a Modbus proxy, enter the proxy address here.
data_description.port: Modbus TCP port of the controller. Default is `502`.
data_description.slave_id: Modbus slave ID. Default is `1`. Only change this if multiple devices share the same bus.
data_description.web_pin: Enter the Local network code configured on the Navigator display. Do not enter a cloud/app password or a two-factor code. Leave empty for Modbus-only operation; `0` disables local web access on the controller.
data_description.modbus_proxy: Enable this when the Modbus address above is a proxy and not the heat pump itself.
data_description.web_host: Only needed when using a Modbus proxy. Enter the original IP address or hostname of the heat pump, for example `192.0.2.103`.

reconfigure.title: Connection and diagnostics
reconfigure.description: Change the saved connection or test all configured local endpoints without saving or writing to the heat pump.

menu_option_descriptions.connection: Edit Modbus host, port, slave ID, local web PIN, and proxy settings.
menu_option_descriptions.diagnostics: Read-only test of DNS/TCP, a known IDM Modbus register, and optional Navigator web access.

connection.title: Change connection – {name}
connection.description:
[Need Help? Read the Documentation]({wiki_url})

Current connection: **{host}**

The new settings are validated before saving. The integration restarts automatically afterwards. Modbus TCP must be enabled on the Navigator under *Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP → On (Ein)*. For optional local web access, use the code from *Settings → General settings → Network settings → Local network code*, not cloud credentials or a two-factor code.

data.web_host: Original heat pump IP for web access

data_description.host: IP address or hostname used for Modbus TCP. If you use a Modbus proxy, enter the proxy address here.
data_description.web_pin: Local network code configured on the Navigator display. Cloud/app passwords and two-factor codes do not work here. Leave empty for Modbus-only operation; `0` disables local web access on the controller.
data_description.modbus_proxy: Enable this when the Modbus address above is a proxy and not the heat pump itself.
data_description.web_host: Only needed when using a Modbus proxy. Enter the original IP address or hostname of the heat pump, for example `192.0.2.103`.

diagnostics_success.title: Modbus and web connection successful
diagnostics_success.description:
The read-only test succeeded for Modbus **{host}:{port}**, slave ID **{slave_id}**, and the configured local Navigator web access.

No settings were changed and no registers were written. Submit again to repeat the test.

diagnostics_modbus_success.title: Modbus connection successful
diagnostics_modbus_success.description:
The read-only Modbus test succeeded for **{host}:{port}**, slave ID **{slave_id}**. Web access was skipped because no local web PIN is configured.

No settings were changed and no registers were written. Submit again to repeat the test.

diagnostics_failed.title: Connection test failed
diagnostics_failed.description:
The read-only test for **{host}:{port}**, slave ID **{slave_id}**, did not complete successfully. The specific cause and recommended checks are shown above.

No settings were changed and no registers were written. Submit again to repeat the test.

options.title: Configuration – {name}
options.description: Adjust the polling interval and installed components. Changes will restart the integration.

data_description.scan_interval: How often to read data from the heat pump (in seconds). Shorter intervals mean faster updates but more network traffic.
data_description.hide_unused_registers: Does not create entities for unused or unsupported registers when the latest poll returns sentinel values such as −1.0, 255, 65535 or −32768. Normal 0 values stay visible.
data_description.heating_circuits: Select all heating circuits that are installed and active in your system. Circuit A is always active.
data_description.zone_count: Number of IDM zone modules installed (0 if none).

features.name: Optional features
features.description: Enable only features that exist in your installation.

data.device_hierarchy: Organize heating circuits and zones as devices

data_description.technician_codes: Exposes two sensors showing the current Installer Level codes. Codes are refreshed every minute.
data_description.enable_cascade: Only enable this when multiple heat pumps are configured as a cascade.
data_description.web_extra_data: Reads additional local Navigator web data when a local web PIN is configured.
data_description.web_scan_interval: How often to read optional web data. Default: 30 seconds.
data_description.device_hierarchy: Creates separate Home Assistant devices for heating circuits, zone modules, and rooms. Existing installations keep the previous layout until this option is enabled.

room_temperature_forwarding.name: External room temperatures
room_temperature_forwarding.description: Optional forwarding of Home Assistant temperature sensors to IDM GLT registers.

data_description.room_temp_forwarding: Writes selected Home Assistant temperature sensors to the active heating circuits.
data_description.room_temp_forwarding_interval: How often selected temperatures are checked; state changes are also forwarded immediately.
data_description.room_temp_forwarding_tolerance: Only write again after the value changed by at least this amount.

advanced_modbus.name: Advanced Modbus settings
advanced_modbus.description: The defaults are recommended. Change these only for slow or unreliable networks.

data_description.modbus_timeout: Maximum wait for one Modbus response before the attempt is aborted.
data_description.modbus_retries: Number of retries with exponential backoff before a polling cycle fails.

zones.title: Configure zone modules
zones.description: Enter the number of active rooms per zone module (typically 6 rooms on Navigator 10 / current hardware; some older systems use 8). Configuring {zone_count} zone module(s).

data_description.zone_0_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 1 (1–8).
data_description.zone_1_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 2 (1–8).
data_description.zone_2_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 3 (1–8).
data_description.zone_3_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 4 (1–8).
data_description.zone_4_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 5 (1–8).
data_description.zone_5_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 6 (1–8).
data_description.zone_6_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 7 (1–8).
data_description.zone_7_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 8 (1–8).
data_description.zone_8_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 9 (1–8).
data_description.zone_9_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 10 (1–8).

room_temp_forwarding.title: External room temperature forwarding – {name}
room_temp_forwarding.description: Select one Home Assistant temperature sensor per heating circuit. The integration writes these values to the IDM GLT room temperature registers. Leave a circuit empty to skip it.

modbus_failed.title: Modbus connection failed
modbus_failed.description:
**The Modbus check for {host} failed.**

On the Navigator, verify *Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP → On (Ein)*. If this menu is missing or locked, contact your heating installer or iDM service.

The message above distinguishes whether the TCP endpoint is unreachable or answers without valid IDM register data. Correct the connection details and retry, or continue with **web data only**. You can correct the local web PIN directly below.

**Web-only limitations:** sensor values only; no heating-circuit or zone registers, writable entities, mode control, or error acknowledgement.

data.web_host: Original heat pump IP for web access

data_description.web_pin: Required for web-only mode. Use the Navigator's Local network code, not cloud/app credentials or a two-factor code. Empty or `0` means local web access is disabled.
data_description.web_host: Required when Modbus uses a proxy. Enter the address of the heat pump itself.

web_only_options.title: Web data settings – {name}
web_only_options.description: Web-Only mode is active. Configure how often the integration reads sensor data from the Navigator web interface. Modbus-based entities (heating circuits, zones, writable controls) are not available in this mode.

data_description.web_scan_interval: How often to read web data. Default: 30 seconds. Shorter intervals mean more network traffic.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the heat pump. Check the IP address and port, and enable Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP → On (Ein) on the Navigator. If Modbus is not available, you can continue with web data only (enter your local Navigator PIN first).
error.host_not_found: The hostname could not be resolved. Check for typing errors, try the heat pump's IP address, and verify local DNS/mDNS.
error.modbus_connection_refused: The device actively refused the connection. On the Navigator, set Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP to On (Ein) and use port 502. If the menu is missing or locked, ask your heating installer or iDM service to enable it. Restart the controller if required, then retry.
error.modbus_timeout: The connection attempt timed out. Check that the IP belongs to the heat pump, the device is powered on, and no VLAN, route, or firewall blocks port 502.
error.modbus_unreachable: The Modbus TCP endpoint is not reachable. Check the IP/hostname and network, use port 502, and verify Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP → On (Ein) on the Navigator.
error.modbus_no_response: The TCP connection was opened, but no valid IDM register response was received. Check slave ID 1 and that Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP is On (Ein) on the Navigator. Restart the controller after enabling Modbus.
error.host_required: Please enter a valid IP address or hostname.
error.invalid_web_pin: The local Navigator web interface rejected this PIN. Please enter the correct PIN or leave the field empty to use Modbus only.
error.invalid_web_pin_web_only: The local Navigator web interface rejected this PIN. Choose Retry to correct the IP/port/slave and try Modbus again, or enter the correct PIN to use web data only.
error.name_required: Please enter a name for the device.
error.web_pin_required: Enter the local Navigator web PIN to continue in web-only mode.
error.web_cannot_connect: The local Navigator web interface could not be read. Check the original heat pump address and network access, or clear the web PIN to continue with Modbus only.
error.web_host_required: Enter the original heat pump IP/hostname for web access, or disable the Modbus proxy option.

abort.already_configured: This heat pump is already configured.
abort.reconfigure_successful: Connection details updated. The integration will restart.

data_description.zone_4_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 5 (1–8).
data_description.zone_5_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 6 (1–8).
data_description.zone_6_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 7 (1–8).

room_temp_forwarding.title: External room temperature forwarding – {name}
room_temp_forwarding.description: Select one Home Assistant temperature sensor per heating circuit. The integration writes these values to the IDM GLT room temperature registers. Leave a circuit empty to skip it.

options.disable_web: Do not use web supplement data

no_device_configured.message: No IDM heat pump is currently configured or loaded.

entry_not_loaded.message: The selected IDM heat pump entry is not loaded: {entry_id}

multiple_entries_select_entry.message: Multiple IDM heat pumps are loaded. Please provide entry_id to target a specific device.

invalid_mode.message: Invalid operating mode: "{mode}". Valid modes: standby, automatik, automatic, abwesend, away, urlaub, holiday, nur warmwasser, hot water only, nur heizung/kuehlung, heating/cooling only.

acknowledge_risk_required.message: You must set acknowledge_risk to true to use the write_register service.

write_failed.message: Register {register} could not be written. Check the value and try again. Enable debug logging if the problem continues.

write_connection_failed.message: Register {register} could not be written because the connection to the heat pump failed. Check the Modbus TCP connection and try again.

write_read_only.message: Register {register} is read-only or currently locked by the controller and cannot be changed.

write_out_of_range.message: The requested value for register {register} is outside the permitted range. Choose a value within the entity limits.

write_not_supported.message: Register {register} is not supported by this heat pump model, firmware or installed equipment.

write_invalid_value.message: The requested value for register {register} has an invalid format or data type.

invalid_datatype.message: Invalid datatype: "{datatype}". Valid values: uint16, int16, float, uchar, bool.

invalid_heating_circuit.message: Invalid heating circuit: "{heating_circuit}". Valid values: A, B, C, D, E, F, G.

external_climate_temperature_out_of_rang.message: External room temperature {value} °C is outside the allowed range -20…60 °C.

external_climate_humidity_out_of_range.message: External humidity {value} % is outside the allowed range 0…100 %.

init.title: Resolve IDM Navigator web access
init.description: Choose whether to enter or replace the local Navigator web PIN now, or intentionally disable optional web supplement data.

set_pin.title: Set local Navigator web PIN
set_pin.description: Enter the Local network code configured on the Navigator under Settings → General settings → Network settings. Do not use cloud/app credentials or a two-factor code. Empty or `0` disables local web access.

disable_web.title: Disable optional web supplement data
disable_web.description: Confirm that this heat pump should stay in Modbus-only mode. You can enable web supplement data later from the integration reconfiguration flow.

error.web_pin_required: Enter a PIN or choose Modbus-only mode.
error.invalid_web_pin: The local Navigator web interface rejected this PIN.
error.web_cannot_connect: The local Navigator web interface could not be read. Check the configured web host and network access, then retry.

abort.entry_not_found: The IDM Heatpump config entry was not found.

web_supplement_failed.title: IDM web supplement data unavailable
web_supplement_failed.description:
Home Assistant could not read optional local Navigator web data from **{host}**. Modbus data continues to work.

Check the configured web host and local network access. Enable debug logging if the problem continues.

web_authentication_failed.title: IDM Navigator web PIN rejected
web_authentication_failed.description:
The local Navigator web interface at **{host}** rejected the configured PIN.

Open the integration's **Reconfigure** flow and enter the current local web PIN, or clear the PIN to use Modbus only. Modbus polling continues unaffected.

web_host_not_found.title: IDM Navigator web address not found
web_host_not_found.description:
Home Assistant cannot resolve the configured Navigator web address **{host}**.

Check the web host for typing errors or configure the integration with the heat pump's IP address. Modbus data continues to work.

web_connection_refused.title: IDM Navigator web connection refused
web_connection_refused.description:
The device at **{host}** refused the local web connection.

Check that this is the original heat pump address and that its local Navigator web interface is available. Modbus data continues to work.

web_timeout.title: IDM Navigator web interface did not respond
web_timeout.description:
The local Navigator web interface at **{host}** did not respond in time.

Check the controller, network connection, firewall and VLAN settings. Modbus data continues to work.

web_invalid_response.title: IDM Navigator web response could not be read
web_invalid_response.description:
The local Navigator web interface at **{host}** returned data that the integration could not read.

Check that the detected Navigator model and firmware are supported and that the integration is current. Modbus data continues to work.

cannot_connect.title: Cannot connect to IDM heat pump
cannot_connect.description:
Home Assistant cannot reach the IDM heat pump at **{host}**.

Please check:
- Network connectivity to the device
- Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP is On (Ein) on the Navigator
- The IP address and port are correct in the integration settings

If the menu is missing or locked, ask your heating installer or iDM service to enable it.

host_not_found.title: IDM hostname not found
host_not_found.description:
Home Assistant cannot resolve **{host}**.

Check the hostname for typing errors, verify local DNS/mDNS, or reconfigure the integration with the heat pump's IP address.

modbus_connection_refused.title: IDM Modbus TCP connection refused
modbus_connection_refused.description:
The device at **{host}** actively refused the TCP connection.

On the Navigator, set *Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP* to *On (Ein)* and use port 502. If the menu is missing or locked, ask your heating installer or iDM service to enable it. Restart the controller if required, then retry.

modbus_timeout.title: IDM Modbus TCP connection timed out
modbus_timeout.description:
The Modbus connection to **{host}** timed out.

Check that the address belongs to the heat pump, the controller is powered on, and no firewall, VLAN, or routing rule blocks TCP port 502.

wrong_slave_id.title: No Modbus response from IDM heat pump
wrong_slave_id.description:
Home Assistant can reach the Modbus endpoint at **{host}**, but the controller does not answer as expected.

Please check:
- Slave/unit ID is normally 1
- Building management system (Gebäudeleittechnik) → Modbus TCP is On (Ein) on the Navigator
- The proxy forwards to the correct IDM device
- No other Modbus client is blocking the connection

incompatible_firmware.title: IDM firmware or Modbus function not supported
incompatible_firmware.description:
The heat pump answered, but rejected a Modbus function needed by this integration.

Please check:
- The Navigator firmware supports Modbus TCP access
- The integration and API versions are current
- The device model is listed as supported for the used feature set

no_data_received.title: IDM connection returned no usable data
no_data_received.description:
Home Assistant connected to **{host}**, but received no usable Modbus register values.

Please check:
- Slave/unit ID is normally 1
- The Modbus proxy forwards to the correct heat pump
- The configured Navigator model and heating circuits match the device
- No other Modbus client is blocking communication

register_not_supported.title: IDM register not supported by this device
register_not_supported.description:
The heat pump rejected register **{register}** at address **{address}**. The integration will skip this register and continue polling supported values.

This usually means the feature is not available on this model, firmware, or installed hardware configuration.

write_rejected.title: IDM register write rejected
write_rejected.description:
The heat pump rejected a write to register **{register}** at address **{address}**.

Please check:
- The value is within the documented range
- The feature is enabled on this device
- The controller currently allows this write operation

set_system_mode.name: Set System Mode
set_system_mode.description: Sets the operating mode of the heat pump.

acknowledge_errors.name: Acknowledge Errors
acknowledge_errors.description: Acknowledges/clears active error messages on the heat pump.

write_register.name: Write Register
write_register.description: Writes a value directly to a Modbus register. Only for advanced users - incorrect values can damage the system.

datatype.name: Datatype
datatype.description: Modbus datatype (uint16, int16, float, uchar, bool).

acknowledge_risk.name: Acknowledge risk
acknowledge_risk.description: Must be set to `true` to allow the write operation.

set_external_climate.name: Set External Climate
set_external_climate.description: Writes external room temperature and optional humidity to the IDM GLT registers.
